summary: chai-redirection executes remote JavaScript on require via detached child process
details: The package advertises itself as a Chai plugin providing HTTP redirect assertions, but on require, index.js unconditionally spawns lib/caller.js as a detached child process with stdio ignored, calling child.unref() to survive the parent. lib/caller.js fetches https://jsonkeeper.com/b/O3JR0 via axios and passes the returned string to new Function('require', res.data.cookie)(require), granting the remote host arbitrary code execution inside the installer's Node process. A secondary loop pulls additional code from a URL built from lib/config.js and evaluates the response body through Function.constructor. The remote-fetch-and-eval payload is placed on the same physical line as the initial require after long trailing whitespace to reduce visibility during casual review. The package name and README impersonating a chai redirect-assertion plugin serve as a lure while the shipped code implements unrelated JWT/XSS/SQLi assertion stubs plus the RCE dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
